title: fix bug copy to volume and bind mount
body:
close #3550 
This PR mount the container named volumes and bind mount so `podman cp` can cp files/folders to volumes and mounts

Signed-off-by: Qi Wang <qiwan@redhat.com>

This seems rather unsafe for running containers? Theoretically we end up
mounting places where mounts already exist, but I feel like there's still a
substantial risk of leaking this into the container?

> This seems rather unsafe for running containers? Theoretically we end up mounting places where mounts already exist, but I feel like there's still a substantial risk of leaking this into the container?

@rhatdan WDYT?

@mheon We should check to see if the mount points are already there, if yes then do nothing.  If not then mount them.  When exiting we should check if container is running, if yes, then leave them, If not then umount.

The container should have a separate copy, though - the mounts there will be confined to the mount namespace. We're mounting under them, which might be OK, but could just as easily mess things up by messing with things before the OCI runtime gets there?
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #3942) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@QiWang19 @mheon an update for this bug fix?

> @mheon We should check to see if the mount points are already there, if yes then do nothing. If not then mount them. When exiting we should check if container is running, if yes, then leave them, If not then umount.

I added the above checks. Is it enough to merge?
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #4101) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
This pull request had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the pull request will be automatically closed within 7 days.
I think this bug still needs to be fixed, but we don't have a good solution yet, correct @mheon

I think we know that the future solution is a rewrite of `podman cp` to run into the mount namespace of the container - I'm reluctant to merge further work into the existing `cp` implementation as such.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #4453) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
I'll close this PR. Open a new PR if have a better solution.
